textbook_example: I was waiting at the railway station when I heard a notice.
speaking_example: The new railway connects the two cities.

textbook_example: There was a heavy rainstorm, so all train services were stopped.
speaking_example: Don't go out — there will be a rainstorm tonight.

textbook_example: All train services were stopped.
speaking_example: The bus service stops at 11 pm.

textbook_example: Others were calling their families or searching for news reports.
speaking_example: I searched the Internet for information about tsunamis.

textbook_example: The music of "Me and My Motherland" stopped all the noise.
speaking_example: We all love our motherland.

chinese_meaning: 剧烈的；厉害的；非常严重的
textbook_example: It was a terrible… (Useful expressions)
speaking_example: It was a terrible earthquake — many houses fell down.

textbook_example: She sensed the danger and warned others.
speaking_example: Keep away from the river — it's full of danger after the flood.

textbook_example: It's been many years since a tsunami happened in Italy.
speaking_example: A tsunami can move very fast across the sea.

textbook_example: What do you know about disaster warnings?
speaking_example: The weather warning said a typhoon was coming.

textbook_example: Iron girl (课文标题)
speaking_example: She has an iron will and never gives up.

textbook_example: During the 2021 Shanghai Fashion Week, a young woman drew everyone's attention.
speaking_example: My sister is really into fashion.

textbook_example: A young, slim and confident woman with an iron leg drew everyone's attention.
speaking_example: She stays slim by running every day.

textbook_example: It wasn't comfortable, and her leg often bled.
speaking_example: My finger is bleeding — I need a plaster.

textbook_example: People's curious eyes made her even more uncomfortable.
speaking_example: These new shoes are really uncomfortable.

textbook_example: She always tried to hide her leg under jeans or dresses.
speaking_example: I usually wear jeans and a T-shirt at weekends.

chinese_meaning: 令人鼓舞的，振奋人心的
textbook_example: To her surprise, she received lots of encouraging words.
speaking_example: My teacher's encouraging words made me feel confident.

textbook_example: She completed the hard 10-year journey of finding herself again.
speaking_example: She made this cake all by herself.

textbook_example: She carried the torch at the FISU World University Games.
speaking_example: My cousin studies at a university in Beijing.

textbook_example: "It is a great honour for me," she said.
speaking_example: It's an honour to speak for our class.

textbook_example: A disaster can bring wounds and pain.
speaking_example: Time can heal the wounds of the heart.

textbook_example: Above all, be the heroine of your life, not the victim.
speaking_example: Mulan is the heroine of the story.
